metadata.methodology: Local Housing Allowance shortfall figures represent the average gap between the LHA rate payable for a given property size and area, and the median market rent for equivalent properties in that area, as calculated by Shelter and JRF using DWP LHA rates and ONS/VOA private rental market data. Recipient figures are DWP benefit units in the private rented sector receiving housing benefit or the housing cost element of Universal Credit.
knownIssues: LHA was reset to the 30th percentile of local rents in April 2024; the 2024 shortfall figure reflects the post-reset average and is not directly comparable to 2016-2023 figures
Shortfall varies substantially by area: in inner London the pre-2024 shortfall was over £500/month; in some northern towns it was minimal

Recipient numbers include some households who moved from Housing Benefit to Universal Credit housing costs during 2019-2023; the two systems have slightly different rules, creating a break in the series
